title: Ordering at a HK bakery
description: Order buns fresh out of the oven and pay with Octopus
context: Hong Kong bakeries (餅店) are a blend of Chinese and Western influences. Pineapple buns (菠蘿包) are arguably HK's most iconic pastry — despite the name, there's no pineapple in them. Wife cakes (老婆餅) are a sweet flaky pastry with winter melon filling.
cultural_note: The pineapple bun (菠蘿包, bo1 lo4 baau1) has no pineapple in it. The cracked golden crust just happens to look like pineapple skin. Split while hot and stuffed with a thick slab of cold butter, it was named a UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage of Hong Kong in 2014, alongside dim sum and milk tea.

lines.speaker: you
lines.jyutping: m4 goi1, bo1 lo4 baau1 zung6 jau5 mou5 aa3?
lines.english: Excuse me, do you still have pineapple buns?

lines.speaker: staff
lines.cjk: 有，啱啱出爐，嗰邊自己夾。
lines.jyutping: jau5, ngaam1 ngaam1 ceot1 lou4, go2 bin1 zi6 gei2 gaap3.
lines.english: Yes, just out of the oven. Help yourself over there.

lines.speaker: you
lines.jyutping: hou2 je5. ni1 di1 hai6 me1 baau1 lai4 gaa3?

lines.speaker: staff
lines.cjk: 雞尾包，入面有椰絲，好香㗎。
lines.jyutping: gai1 mei5 baau1, jap6 min6 jau5 je4 si1, hou2 hoeng1 gaa3.
lines.english: Cocktail buns, coconut filling. Really fragrant.

lines.speaker: you
lines.cjk: 要兩個菠蘿包，一個雞尾包，唔該。
lines.jyutping: jiu3 loeng5 go3 bo1 lo4 baau1, jat1 go3 gai1 mei5 baau1, m4 goi1.
lines.english: Two pineapple buns and one cocktail bun please.

lines.speaker: you
lines.jyutping: baat3 daat6 tung1 dak1 m4 dak1?
